questions.text: If $f(x) = x^2 + 3$, then use the definition of derivative to find f'(x)?
questions.explanation: Using the power rule, f'(x)=2x. None of the provided options equals 2x.

questions.text: If $y = (2x + 1)(x + 2)(x + 3)$, then find the derivative of y with respect to x.
questions.explanation: Expand: (x+2)(x+3)=x^2+5x+6; multiply by (2x+1) to get y=2x^3+11x^2+17x+6. Differentiate to get y'=6x^2+22x+17.
The derivative of sin(x) with respect to x is cos(x).
